title: play kube: service container
body:
Add the notion of a "service container" to play kube.  A service
container is started before the pods in play kube and is (reverse)
linked to them.  The service container is stopped/removed *after*
all pods it is associated with are stopped/removed.

In other words, a service container tracks the entire life cycle
of a service started via `podman play kube`.  This is required to
enable `play kube` in a systemd unit file.

The service container is only used when the `--service-container`
flag is set on the CLI.  This flag has been marked as hidden as it
is not meant to be used outside the context of `play kube`.  It is
further not supported on the remote client.

The wiring with systemd will be done in a later commit.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@redhat.com>

Release notes come along with the systemd integration. As mentioned above, this is not something I want users to know or worry about.

```release-note
None
```

@rhatdan @mheon PTAL

That's the design we discussed last week.
Nice work.  Can you make sure that I can podman rm SERVICECONTAINER gives a decent error message.  Also is there something in podman ps to indicate what this container is? podman inspect?

> Can you make sure that I can podman rm SERVICECONTAINER gives a decent error message. 

Something down the line of "cannot remove service container because pod A, B, C are not removed" ?

> Also is there something in podman ps to indicate what this container is?

Service containers will have the `-service` suffix in their name.  Similar to the `-infra` suffix for infra containers.  The ID before the suffix are the first 12 characters of the _hash_ of the input YAML file.  This way, the names are predicatable.

> podman inspect?

Shall we expose the IsService field?
Yes expose the isService field.
